name: Fora Financial
description_short: Fora Financial provides small business term loans up to $1.5M, revenue advances, lines of credit, SBA loans, and equipment financing with published factor rates, 4-hour approvals, and 24-hour funding for businesses with 570+ FICO scores.
description_long:
Fora Financial, formerly known as Paramount Merchant Funding, has been providing working capital to small and mid-sized businesses since 2008. Headquartered at 1385 Broadway, 15th Floor in New York City, the company has funded over $5 billion to more than 55,000 businesses across the United States, earning a spot on the Inc. 5000 list six consecutive years.

The company offers five core products: term loans up to $1.5 million, revenue advances, lines of credit ranging from $5,000 to $100,000, SBA loans, and equipment financing. Factor rates range from 1.13 to 1.50 depending on loan term and risk profile, with origination fees starting at 2.5%. Rather than penalizing early repayment, Fora Financial offers early payoff discounts that can reduce the effective factor rate to as low as 1.05 — a significant incentive that sets it apart from competitors who lock borrowers into full-cost structures.

Fora Financial performs only a soft credit pull during the application process, preserving borrowers' credit scores. The minimum FICO requirement is 570, the lowest among comparable business lenders. Businesses need at least six months of operating history and approximately $204,000 in annual revenue ($17,000 per month) to qualify. Approval decisions come in as little as four hours, with funds deposited within 24 hours.

Since 2016, Palladium Equity Partners has held a majority stake in the company. Fora Financial originates loans through Celtic Bank and third-party lending partners. The company serves a wide range of industries including automotive, construction, eCommerce, manufacturing, medical, restaurants, retail, services, transportation, and wholesale.

It is worth noting that Fora Financial does not report payment activity to business credit bureaus, so on-time payments will not help build your business credit profile. Stacking additional loans from other providers violates the loan agreement, and funds cannot be used to purchase an existing business. Despite these limitations, the combination of published rates, early payoff savings, low credit requirements, and rapid funding makes Fora Financial a competitive option for businesses needing fast working capital. The company holds an A+ rating with the Better Business Bureau.

For business owners exploring financing options, the landscape includes several distinct products. Small business loans provide lump-sum working capital with fixed repayment terms, while a business line of credit offers flexible revolving access to funds as needed. SBA loans through government-backed programs offer the lowest rates but require longer approval timelines. For businesses with outstanding invoices, invoice factoring converts receivables into immediate cash flow. Equipment financing allows companies to acquire machinery and technology with the equipment itself as collateral. A merchant cash advance provides fast business funding by purchasing a share of future sales, though typically at higher effective costs. Startups and newer businesses may find working capital loan options through alternative lenders with lower qualification requirements than traditional banks.
meta_description: Fora Financial review 2026: term loans up to $1.5M, factor rates 1.13-1.50, 570 min FICO, 4-hour approval, 24-hour funding. $5B+ funded since 2008. A+ BBB rated.
pros: Publishes factor rates (1.13-1.50) unlike most competitors who hide pricing
Early repayment discounts reduce effective cost to as low as 1.05 factor
570 minimum FICO — one of the lowest thresholds in business lending
4-hour approval with funds deposited within 24 hours
Soft credit pull only — no impact on personal credit score
$5B+ funded to 55,000+ businesses with A+ BBB rating and Inc. 5000 six years
cons: Does not report to business credit bureaus — on-time payments won't build business credit
Stacking loans from other providers violates the contract terms
Cannot use funds to purchase an existing business
Lines of credit capped at $100K — significantly less than term loan max
Factor rate of 1.50 on 24-month terms translates to a very high effective APR

tiers.name: Line of Credit
features: Pay only on what you use

best_for: Businesses with credit scores as low as 570
Companies needing fast funding within 24 hours
Borrowers who may repay early and want discount incentives

features.industries_served: Auto, Construction, eCommerce, Manufacturing, Medical, Restaurants, Retail, Services, Transportation, Wholesale
